text: The surgeon adjusts the morcellator from safe mode to cut mode using a foot switch, then switches from a grasper to a tenaculum for better grip on a fibroid. The tenaculum provides a secure hold without hitting the blade, allowing effective cutting of the fibroid tissue.

The surgeon uses a new morselator blade to effectively cut the fibroid. Alternative removal methods include bisecting the fibroid with two tooth needle holders and extracting it through a ten mm trocar or performing a colpotomy from the posterior pouch for removal.
